verses.sanskrit: विदुर उवाचभवे शीलवतां श्रेष्ठे दक्षो दुहितृवत्सल: ।विद्वेषमकरोत्कस्मादनाद‍ृत्यात्मजां सतीम् ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: vidura uvācabhave śīlavatāṁ śreṣṭhedakṣo duhitṛ-vatsalaḥvidveṣam akarot kasmādanādṛtyātmajāṁ satīm
verses.text: Vidura inquired: Why was Dakṣa, who was so affectionate towards his daughter, envious of Lord Śiva, who is the best among the gentle? Why did he neglect his daughter Satī?

verses.sanskrit: कस्तं चराचरगुरुं निर्वैरं शान्तविग्रहम् ।आत्मारामं कथं द्वेष्टि जगतो दैवतं महत् ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: kas taṁ carācara-guruṁnirvairaṁ śānta-vigrahamātmārāmaṁ kathaṁ dveṣṭijagato daivataṁ mahat
verses.text: Lord Śiva, the spiritual master of the entire world, is free from enmity, is a peaceful personality, and is always satisfied in himself. He is the greatest among the demigods. How is it possible that Dakṣa could be inimical towards such an auspicious personality?

verses.sanskrit: एतदाख्याहि मे ब्रह्मन्जामातु: श्वशुरस्य च ।विद्वेषस्तु यत: प्राणांस्तत्यजे दुस्त्यजान्सती ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: etad ākhyāhi me brahmanjāmātuḥ śvaśurasya cavidveṣas tu yataḥ prāṇāṁstatyaje dustyajān satī
verses.text: My dear Maitreya, to part with one’s life is very difficult. Would you kindly explain to me how such a son-in-law and father-in-law could quarrel so bitterly that the great goddess Satī could give up her life?

verses.sanskrit: मैत्रेय उवाचपुरा विश्वसृजां सत्रे समेता: परमर्षय: ।तथामरगणा: सर्वे सानुगा मुनयोऽग्नय: ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: maitreya uvācapurā viśva-sṛjāṁ satresametāḥ paramarṣayaḥtathāmara-gaṇāḥ sarvesānugā munayo ’gnayaḥ
verses.text: The sage Maitreya said: In a former time, the leaders of the universal creation performed a great sacrifice in which all the great sages, philosophers, demigods and fire-gods assembled with their followers.

verses.sanskrit: तत्र प्रविष्टमृषयो दृष्ट्वार्कमिव रोचिषा ।भ्राजमानं वितिमिरं कुर्वन्तं तन्महत्सद: ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatra praviṣṭam ṛṣayodṛṣṭvārkam iva rociṣābhrājamānaṁ vitimiraṁkurvantaṁ tan mahat sadaḥ
verses.text: When Dakṣa, the leader of the Prajāpatis, entered that assembly, his personal bodily luster as bright as the effulgence of the sun, the entire assembly was illuminated, and all the assembled personalities became insignificant in his presence.

verses.sanskrit: उदतिष्ठन्सदस्यास्ते स्वधिष्ण्येभ्य: सहाग्नय: ।ऋते विरिञ्चां शर्वं च तद्भासाक्षिप्तचेतस: ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: udatiṣṭhan sadasyās tesva-dhiṣṇyebhyaḥ sahāgnayaḥṛte viriñcāṁ śarvaṁ catad-bhāsākṣipta-cetasaḥ
verses.text: Influenced by his personal bodily luster, all the fire-gods and other participants in that great assembly, with the exceptions of Lord Brahmā and Lord Śiva, gave up their own sitting places and stood in respect for Dakṣa.

verses.sanskrit: सदसस्पतिभिर्दक्षो भगवान्साधु सत्कृत: ।अजं लोकगुरुं नत्वा निषसाद तदाज्ञया ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: sadasas-patibhir dakṣobhagavān sādhu sat-kṛtaḥajaṁ loka-guruṁ natvāniṣasāda tad-ājñayā
verses.text: Dakṣa was adequately welcomed by the president of the great assembly, Lord Brahmā. After offering Lord Brahmā respect, Dakṣa, by the order of Brahmā, properly took his seat.

verses.sanskrit: प्राङ्‍‌निषण्णं मृडं दृष्ट्वा नामृष्यत्तदनाद‍ृत: ।उवाच वामं चक्षुर्भ्यामभिवीक्ष्य दहन्निव ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: prāṅ-niṣaṇṇaṁ mṛḍaṁ dṛṣṭvānāmṛṣyat tad-anādṛtaḥuvāca vāmaṁ cakṣurbhyāmabhivīkṣya dahann iva
verses.text: Before taking his seat, however, Dakṣa was very much offended to see Lord Śiva sitting and not showing him any respect. At that time, Dakṣa became greatly angry, and, his eyes glowing, he began to speak very strongly against Lord Śiva.

verses.sanskrit: श्रूयतां ब्रह्मर्षयो मे सहदेवा: सहाग्नय: ।साधूनां ब्रुवतो वृत्तं नाज्ञानान्न च मत्सरात् ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrūyatāṁ brahmarṣayo mesaha-devāḥ sahāgnayaḥsādhūnāṁ bruvato vṛttaṁnājñānān na ca matsarāt
verses.text: All sages, brāhmaṇas and fire-gods present, please hear me with attention, for I speak about the manners of gentle persons. I do not speak out of ignorance or envy.

verses.sanskrit: अयं तु लोकपालानां यशोघ्नो निरपत्रप: ।सद्‌भिराचरित: पन्था येन स्तब्धेन दूषित: ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: ayaṁ tu loka-pālānāṁyaśo-ghno nirapatrapaḥsadbhir ācaritaḥ panthāyena stabdhena dūṣitaḥ
verses.text: Śiva has spoiled the name and fame of the governors of the universe and has polluted the path of gentle manners. Because he is shameless, he does not know how to act.

verses.sanskrit: एष मे शिष्यतां प्राप्तो यन्मे दुहितुरग्रहीत् ।पाणिं विप्राग्निमुखत: सावित्र्या इव साधुवत् ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: eṣa me śiṣyatāṁ prāptoyan me duhitur agrahītpāṇiṁ viprāgni-mukhataḥsāvitryā iva sādhuvat
verses.text: He has already accepted himself as my subordinate by marrying my daughter in the presence of fire and brāhmaṇas. He has married my daughter, who is equal to Gāyatrī, and has pretended to be just like an honest person.

verses.sanskrit: गृहीत्वा मृगशावाक्ष्या: पाणिं मर्कटलोचन: ।प्रत्युत्थानाभिवादार्हे वाचाप्यकृत नोचितम् ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: gṛhītvā mṛga-śāvākṣyāḥpāṇiṁ markaṭa-locanaḥpratyutthānābhivādārhevācāpy akṛta nocitam
verses.text: He has eyes like a monkey’s, yet he has married my daughter, whose eyes are just like those of a deer cub. Nevertheless he did not stand up to receive me, nor did he think it fit to welcome me with sweet words.

verses.sanskrit: लुप्तक्रियायाशुचये मानिने भिन्नसेतवे ।अनिच्छन्नप्यदां बालां शूद्रायेवोशतीं गिरम् ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: lupta-kriyāyāśucayemānine bhinna-setaveanicchann apy adāṁ bālāṁśūdrāyevośatīṁ giram
verses.text: I had no desire to give my daughter to this person, who has broken all rules of civility. Because of not observing the required rules and regulations, he is impure, but I was obliged to hand over my daughter to him just as one teaches the messages of the Vedas to a śūdra.

verses.sanskrit: प्रेतावासेषु घोरेषु प्रेतैर्भूतगणैर्वृत: ।अटत्युन्मत्तवन्नग्नो व्युप्तकेशो हसन् रुदन् ॥ १४ ॥चिताभस्मकृतस्‍नान: प्रेतस्रङ्‌न्रस्थिभूषण: ।शिवापदेशो ह्यशिवो मत्तो मत्तजनप्रिय: ।पति: प्रमथनाथानां तमोमात्रात्मकात्मनाम् ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: pretāvāseṣu ghoreṣupretair bhūta-gaṇair vṛtaḥaṭaty unmattavan nagnovyupta-keśo hasan rudan
verses.text: He lives in filthy places like crematoriums, and his companions are the ghosts and demons. Naked like a madman, sometimes laughing and sometimes crying, he smears crematorium ashes all over his body. He does not bathe regularly, and he ornaments his body with a garland of skulls and bones. Therefore only in name is he śiva, or auspicious; actually, he is the most mad and inauspicious creature. Thus he is very dear to crazy beings in the gross mode of ignorance, and he is their leader.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मा उन्मादनाथाय नष्टशौचाय दुर्हृदे ।दत्ता बत मया साध्वी चोदिते परमेष्ठिना ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasmā unmāda-nāthāyanaṣṭa-śaucāya durhṛdedattā bata mayā sādhvīcodite parameṣṭhinā
verses.text: On the request of Lord Brahmā I handed over my chaste daughter to him, although he is devoid of all cleanliness and his heart is filled with nasty things.

verses.sanskrit: मैत्रेय उवाचविनिन्द्यैवं स गिरिशमप्रतीपमवस्थितम् ।दक्षोऽथाप उपस्पृश्य क्रुद्ध: शप्तुं प्रचक्रमे ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: maitreya uvācavinindyaivaṁ sa giriśamapratīpam avasthitamdakṣo ’thāpa upaspṛśyakruddhaḥ śaptuṁ pracakrame
verses.text: The sage Maitreya continued: Thus Dakṣa, seeing Lord Śiva sitting as if against him, washed his hands and mouth and cursed him in the following words.

verses.sanskrit: अयं तु देवयजन इन्द्रोपेन्द्रादिभिर्भव: ।सह भागं न लभतां देवैर्देवगणाधम: ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: ayaṁ tu deva-yajanaindropendrādibhir bhavaḥsaha bhāgaṁ na labhatāṁdevair deva-gaṇādhamaḥ
verses.text: The demigods are eligible to share in the oblations of sacrifice, but Lord Śiva, who is the lowest of all the demigods, should not have a share.

verses.sanskrit: निषिध्यमान: स सदस्यमुख्यै-र्दक्षो गिरित्राय विसृज्य शापम् ।तस्माद्विनिष्क्रम्य विवृद्धमन्यु-र्जगाम कौरव्य निजं निकेतनम् ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: niṣidhyamānaḥ sa sadasya-mukhyairdakṣo giritrāya visṛjya śāpamtasmād viniṣkramya vivṛddha-manyurjagāma kauravya nijaṁ niketanam
verses.text: Maitreya continued: My dear Vidura, in spite of the requests of all the members of the sacrificial assembly, Dakṣa, in great anger, cursed Lord Śiva and then left the assembly and went back to his home.

verses.sanskrit: विज्ञाय शापं गिरिशानुगाग्रणी-र्नन्दीश्वरो रोषकषायदूषित: ।दक्षाय शापं विससर्ज दारुणंये चान्वमोदंस्तदवाच्यतां द्विजा: ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: vijñāya śāpaṁ giriśānugāgraṇīrnandīśvaro roṣa-kaṣāya-dūṣitaḥdakṣāya śāpaṁ visasarja dāruṇaṁye cānvamodaṁs tad-avācyatāṁ dvijāḥ
verses.text: Upon understanding that Lord Śiva had been cursed, Nandīśvara, one of Lord Śiva’s principal associates, became greatly angry. His eyes became red, and he prepared to curse Dakṣa and all the brāhmaṇas present there who had tolerated Dakṣa’s cursing Śiva in harsh words.

verses.sanskrit: य एतन्मर्त्यमुद्दिश्य भगवत्यप्रतिद्रुहि ।द्रुह्यत्यज्ञ: पृथग्दृष्टिस्तत्त्वतो विमुखो भवेत् ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: ya etan martyam uddiśyabhagavaty apratidruhidruhyaty ajñaḥ pṛthag-dṛṣṭistattvato vimukho bhavet
verses.text: Anyone who has accepted Dakṣa as the most important personality and neglected Lord Śiva because of envy is less intelligent and, because of visualizing in duality, will be bereft of transcendental knowledge.

verses.sanskrit: गृहेषु कूटधर्मेषु सक्तो ग्राम्यसुखेच्छया ।कर्मतन्त्रं वितनुते वेदवादविपन्नधी: ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: gṛheṣu kūṭa-dharmeṣusakto grāmya-sukhecchayākarma-tantraṁ vitanuteveda-vāda-vipanna-dhīḥ
verses.text: Pretentiously religious householder life, in which one is attracted to material happiness and thus also attracted to the superficial explanation of the Vedas, robs one of all intelligence and attaches one to fruitive activities as all in all.

verses.sanskrit: बुद्ध्या पराभिध्यायिन्या विस्मृतात्मगति: पशु: ।स्त्रीकाम: सोऽस्त्वतितरां दक्षो बस्तमुखोऽचिरात् ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: buddhyā parābhidhyāyinyāvismṛtātma-gatiḥ paśuḥstrī-kāmaḥ so ’stv atitarāṁdakṣo basta-mukho ’cirāt
verses.text: Dakṣa has accepted the body as all in all. Therefore, since he has forgotten the viṣṇu-pāda, or viṣṇu-gati, and is attached to sex life only, within a short time he will have the face of a goat.

verses.sanskrit: विद्याबुद्धिरविद्यायां कर्ममय्यामसौ जड: ।संसरन्त्विह ये चामुमनु शर्वावमानिनम् ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: vidyā-buddhir avidyāyāṁkarmamayyām asau jaḍaḥsaṁsarantv iha ye cāmumanu śarvāvamāninam
verses.text: Those who have become as dull as matter by cultivating materialistic education and intelligence are nesciently involved in fruitive activities. Such men have purposely insulted Lord Śiva. May they continue in the cycle of repeated birth and death.

verses.sanskrit: गिर: श्रुताया: पुष्पिण्या मधुगन्धेन भूरिणा ।मथ्ना चोन्मथितात्मान: सम्मुह्यन्तु हरद्विष: ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: giraḥ śrutāyāḥ puṣpiṇyāmadhu-gandhena bhūriṇāmathnā conmathitātmānaḥsammuhyantu hara-dviṣaḥ
verses.text: May those who are envious of Lord Śiva, being attracted by the flowery language of the enchanting Vedic promises, and who have thus become dull, always remain attached to fruitive activities.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वभक्षा द्विजा वृत्त्यै धृतविद्यातपोव्रता: ।वित्तदेहेन्द्रियारामा याचका विचरन्त्विह ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: sarva-bhakṣā dvijā vṛttyaidhṛta-vidyā-tapo-vratāḥvitta-dehendriyārāmāyācakā vicarantv iha
verses.text: These brāhmaṇas take to education, austerity and vows only for the purpose of maintaining the body. They shall be devoid of discrimination between what to eat and what not to eat. They will acquire money, begging from door to door, simply for the satisfaction of the body.

verses.sanskrit: तस्यैवं वदत: शापं श्रुत्वा द्विजकुलाय वै ।भृगु: प्रत्यसृजच्छापं ब्रह्मदण्डं दुरत्ययम् ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasyaivaṁ vadataḥ śāpaṁśrutvā dvija-kulāya vaibhṛguḥ pratyasṛjac chāpaṁbrahma-daṇḍaṁ duratyayam
verses.text: When all the hereditary brāhmaṇas were thus cursed by Nandīśvara, the sage Bhṛgu, as a reaction, condemned the followers of Lord Śiva with this very strong brahminical curse.

verses.sanskrit: भवव्रतधरा ये च ये च तान्समनुव्रता: ।पाषण्डिनस्ते भवन्तु सच्छास्त्रपरिपन्थिन: ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhava-vrata-dharā ye caye ca tān samanuvratāḥpāṣaṇḍinas te bhavantusac-chāstra-paripanthinaḥ
verses.text: One who takes a vow to satisfy Lord Śiva or who follows such principles will certainly become an atheist and be diverted from transcendental scriptural injunctions.

verses.sanskrit: नष्टशौचा मूढधियो जटाभस्मास्थिधारिण: ।विशन्तु शिवदीक्षायां यत्र दैवं सुरासवम् ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: naṣṭa-śaucā mūḍha-dhiyojaṭā-bhasmāsthi-dhāriṇaḥviśantu śiva-dīkṣāyāṁyatra daivaṁ surāsavam
verses.text: Those who vow to worship Lord Śiva are so foolish that they imitate him by keeping long hair on their heads. When initiated into worship of Lord Śiva, they prefer to live on wine, flesh and other such things.

verses.sanskrit: ब्रह्म च ब्राह्मणांश्चैव यद्यूयं परिनिन्दथ ।सेतुं विधारणं पुंसामत: पाषण्डमाश्रिता: ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: brahma ca brāhmaṇāṁś caivayad yūyaṁ parinindathasetuṁ vidhāraṇaṁ puṁsāmataḥ pāṣaṇḍam āśritāḥ
verses.text: Bhṛgu Muni continued: Since you blaspheme the Vedas and the brāhmaṇas, who are followers of the Vedic principles, it is understood that you have already taken shelter of the doctrine of atheism.

verses.sanskrit: एष एव हि लोकानां शिव: पन्था: सनातन: ।यं पूर्वे चानुसन्तस्थुर्यत्प्रमाणं जनार्दन: ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: eṣa eva hi lokānāṁśivaḥ panthāḥ sanātanaḥyaṁ pūrve cānusantasthuryat-pramāṇaṁ janārdanaḥ
verses.text: The Vedas give the eternal regulative principles for auspicious advancement in human civilization which have been rigidly followed in the past. The strong evidence of this principle is the Supreme Personality of Godhead, who is called Janārdana, the well-wisher of all living entities.

verses.sanskrit: तद्ब्रह्म परमं शुद्धं सतां वर्त्म सनातनम् ।विगर्ह्य यात पाषण्डं दैवं वो यत्र भूतराट् ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: tad brahma paramaṁ śuddhaṁsatāṁ vartma sanātanamvigarhya yāta pāṣaṇḍaṁdaivaṁ vo yatra bhūta-rāṭ
verses.text: By blaspheming the principles of the Vedas, which are the pure and supreme path of the saintly persons, certainly you followers of Bhūtapati, Lord Śiva, will descend to the standard of atheism without a doubt.

verses.sanskrit: मैत्रेय उवाचतस्यैवं वदत: शापं भृगो: स भगवान् भव: ।निश्चक्राम तत: किञ्चिद्विमना इव सानुग: ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: maitreya uvācatasyaivaṁ vadataḥ śāpaṁbhṛgoḥ sa bhagavān bhavaḥniścakrāma tataḥ kiñcidvimanā iva sānugaḥ
verses.text: The sage Maitreya said: When such cursing and countercursing was going on between Lord Śiva’s followers and the parties of Dakṣa and Bhṛgu, Lord Śiva became very morose. Not saying anything, he left the arena of the sacrifice, followed by his disciples.

verses.sanskrit: तेऽपि विश्वसृज: सत्रं सहस्रपरिवत्सरान् ।संविधाय महेष्वास यत्रेज्य ऋषभो हरि: ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: te ’pi viśva-sṛjaḥ satraṁsahasra-parivatsarānsaṁvidhāya maheṣvāsayatrejya ṛṣabho hariḥ
verses.text: The sage Maitreya continued: O Vidura, all the progenitors of the universal population thus executed a sacrifice for thousands of years, for sacrifice is the best way to worship the Supreme Lord, Hari, the Personality of Godhead.

verses.sanskrit: आप्लुत्यावभृथं यत्र गङ्गा यमुनयान्विता ।विरजेनात्मना सर्वे स्वं स्वं धाम ययुस्तत: ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: āplutyāvabhṛthaṁ yatragaṅgā yamunayānvitāvirajenātmanā sarvesvaṁ svaṁ dhāma yayus tataḥ
verses.text: My dear Vidura, carrier of bows and arrows, all the demigods who were performing the sacrifice took their bath at the confluence of the Ganges and the Yamunā after completing the yajña performance. Such a bath is called avabhṛtha-snāna. After thus becoming purified in heart, they departed for their respective abodes.
